results.title: 😺 ChatGPT in your pocket | Product Hunt
results.content:
Power users will be happy to hear that the mobile app syncs your history across your devices, meaning it can pull the previous conversations you’ve had with the web client and make them accessible – on the go.

Siri has a challenger: With Apple’s seemingly radio silence and lack of progress on the AI front, coupled with Siri’s existing issues, the new ChatGPT presents an interesting future where we might see iPhone users opting to replace Apple’s voice assistant with ChatGPT. [...] ## Are you really still typing?

Newsletter Sponsor

Full disclosure: Wispr Flow is the AI dictation tool most of us at Product Hunt (use we still have a few holdout typers, what romantics). Hold a key, talk, and clean text drops straight into whatever app you're already in — Slack, email, Notion, your IDE, wherever your cursor lives. No switching windows. No copy-paste ritual. Just say the thing – yes, you can whisper it – and even your most run-on sentences will be turned into polished writing at 4x the speed of typing.

The Game Wallet allows you to transform your Gameboy into a crypto wallet.

Twitter Race gamifies your Twitter growth by pitting you against a friend in a growth race.

Rooms.xyz lets you create interactive rooms and games in your browser. [...] AI Chat SMS lets you chat to an AI bot through SMS.

WebWhiz is an open-source, custom ChatGPT bot for your site.

ZBrain allows you to quickly build ChatGPT apps using private data.

CapitalConnector.ai is a search engine for discovering investors for your product.

results.title: Timelaps: Know if your marketing is working with real-time insights | Product Hunt
results.content:
Shawn U.

@dparrelli  @harryzhangs  Really appreciate the transparency on the 4,000/year panel size, Harry. That F500-validated sample size as a benchmark is exactly the kind of rigor that builds trust, especially for challenger brands trying to compete with bigger players who've historically had access to expensive research tools.

The 82-market coverage is impressive too. As someone launching a consumer app (Kinetic Mingle, a video-first dating platform launching March 24 here on PH), understanding how brand perception shifts across different demographics is something I think about constantly. The idea that your AI will eventually recommend optimal panel sizes per market/category is really forward-thinking. [...] @dparrelli Thanks for the question, David! You understood the value prop immediately.   
  
We are keeping panel size consistent for our enterprise and challenger brand clients and design partners now at 4,000 / year (same as other leading self-service tools & functions as a better, cheaper, fully-automated Qualtrics alternative). This is the number widely considered by F500 companies and brand marketing researchers globally as the gold standard for a representative sample size to draw meaningful recommendations over time. [...] Timelaps

# Timelaps

results.title: 🔥 Best AI Automation Tools: Nominate Your Favorites for the Product Hunt Orbit Awards : Product Hunt Discussion Forums | Product Hunt
results.content:
Hovhannes Ghevondyan
Aikido Security

Aikido Security

I really like the idea of @Trace (even though it's kinda rough on implementation side rn). I gave it a messy idea and it came back with a workflow that usually makes sense. Some tasks it flagged as automatable that I hadn’t even thought about. Also nice that I can flip a task back to myself when the AI struggles. Overall really cool idea and I hope it continues to improve!

Christophe HK
Haz Hubble
Pally - AI Relationship Management

Pally - AI Relationship Management

@Trace Very solid idea! Takes some time playing with the tool to get the results, but when it works it's very nice!

Nodari Kolmakhidze [...] This one is for the tools that actually do work for you – clearing chores, running workflows in the background, or quietly taking over a chunk of your week without turning into another dashboard you have to babysit.

We’re digging into the AI Automation category based on traction and reviews, not hype. So we’d love your help:

Drop your favorite AI automation tools in the thread

Add a quick note on what they actually do for you day to day

And if you want them in the running for an Orbit, leave a review on their Product Hunt Hub

Think of this as nominations-by-use-case, not just vibes. Which tools have actually earned a permanent spot in your stack?

55

356

Add a comment

Aleksandar Blazhev
ZeroHuman.

ZeroHuman.

Thank you for the thread @aaronoleary: [...] Let me know if you need any assistance with content / review for any when you write the next Orbit awards post. I am reachable via email anytime. :)

Alex Newman

Claude-Mem

I nominate Claude-Mem but clearly I’m biased :)

Harris Cheng
Jupitrr AI

Jupitrr AI

Nominating @Trace   
Short version: it helps me think. Long version: I throw in a goal, Trace builds a workflow, and I refine it from there. I’ve been using it for posting to Twitter as part of larger workflows. Would love a way to debug agent runs step by step.

Hovhannes Ghevondyan
